channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: eThekwini city manager outlines progress and long-term development strategy for Durban
item.description: eThekwini Municipality city manager Musa Mbhele has defended his leadership amid criticism, arguing that Durban has undergone steady improvement in service delivery, infrastructure development, and in...

item.title: Rescue operations continue after building collapse in Angeles City leaves 2 dead and 18 missing
item.description: A serious building collapse in Angeles City has left at least two people confirmed dead and 18 others still missing, prompting ongoing rescue operations that have now entered their second day. Emergen...

item.title: Delhi Police detain father in suspected murder of missing 10-month-old infant in Mukundpur
item.description: A case initially reported as the disappearance of a 10-month-old infant in Delhi’s Mukundpur area took a serious turn after police detained the child’s father on suspicion of murder. According to poli...

item.title: Ottawa Valley resort offsets rising fuel costs with gas gift card promotion for visitors
item.description: An Ottawa Valley resort is launching a promotional initiative aimed at helping visitors cope with rising fuel prices, which are approaching $2 per litre. The resort is offering gas gift cards as an in...

item.title: Updated List of Kenya’s 47 County Commissioners Following 2026 Government Reshuffle
item.description: The article gives an updated list of all 47 county commissioners serving across Kenya’s counties as of 2026 after the latest reshuffle announced by Interior Cabinet Secretary Kipchumba Murkomen on 15 ...

item.title: Canberra startup founders seek stronger investment support to keep businesses local
item.description: A growing number of Canberra entrepreneurs are trying to build successful startups while remaining based in the ACT, but many say access to investment capital remains the biggest challenge. The articl...

item.title: Arsenal clinch Premier League title after 2-1 win over Crystal Palace
item.description: Arsenal don officially become Premier League champions after dem secure a 2-1 victory over Crystal Palace for Selhurst Park. The result confirm say Mikel Arteta side don win their first English top-fl...

item.title: Agot Isidro set to celebrate birthday with a concert in July
item.description: Filipina singer and actress Agot Isidro is set to mark her birthday with a special concert scheduled for July, as reported by ABS-CBN News. The announcement highlights the upcoming celebratory event, ...

item.title: Delhi Gymkhana members prepare legal challenge against government eviction notice
item.description: Members of the historic Delhi Gymkhana Club have decided to challenge the Union government's eviction order in court after receiving directions to vacate the club premises in Lutyens' Delhi by June 5....

item.title: Poll shows declining concern over Alberta and Quebec separatism in Canada
item.description: A recent survey conducted for the Association for Canadian Studies suggests that Canadians are becoming less concerned about the possibility of separatist movements in both Alberta and Quebec, despite...

item.title: Felix Rosenqvist Wins Historic Closest Finish at the Indianapolis 500
item.description: Felix Rosenqvist secured a dramatic and historic victory at the Indianapolis 500, winning the race by just 0.0233 seconds in the closest finish ever recorded in the event’s history. The Swedish driver...

item.title: Felix Rosenqvist clinches 2026 Indianapolis 500 after dramatic final-lap sprint
item.description: Felix Rosenqvist produced a dramatic late charge to win the 110th Indianapolis 500 in one of the most tightly contested finishes in recent memory. The Swedish driver, aged 34, edged past David Malukas...

item.title: Ten injured after turbulence on Cathay Pacific flight from Brisbane to Hong Kong
item.description: A Cathay Pacific flight travelling from Brisbane to Hong Kong experienced severe turbulence that left ten people injured during a routine meal service. Flight CX156, operated by an Airbus A350-941, en...

item.title: TD focuses on Canadian operations to support growth amid U.S. regulatory constraints
item.description: Toronto-Dominion Bank is increasingly relying on its Canadian operations to drive future growth as it works through significant regulatory and financial setbacks in the United States. After pleading g...

item.title: Rivers State Government Commends Anglican Church for Role in Peace, Education and Social Development
item.description: The Rivers State Government has openly appreciated the Church of Nigeria (Anglican Communion) for its consistent contributions to the development of the state, especially in promoting peace, unity, an...

item.title: Trump Faces Growing Republican Opposition as Iran Peace Negotiations Advance
item.description: The article discusses mounting political tensions within Republican circles as President Donald Trump moves closer to a possible negotiated agreement with Iran aimed at ending the ongoing U.S. conflic...

item.title: Iran Air Flight Attendants and Their Role in Pre-1979 Revolutionary Iran
item.description: Before the 1979 Iranian Revolution, Iran Air, known as Homa after a mythical Persian griffin, stood as one of the world's fastest-growing and most prestigious airlines. Its flight attendants represent...

item.title: Castle Lager launches nationwide search for Bafana Bafana’s most loyal supporter ahead of FIFA World Cup 2026
item.description: South Africa’s qualification for the FIFA World Cup 2026 after a 16-year absence has inspired a nationwide campaign celebrating the country’s most dedicated Bafana Bafana supporters. Castle Lager, one...

item.title: Opinion: Why society should stop glorifying polygamy
item.description: This opinion piece argues that modern society, particularly in Kenya, should reconsider and ultimately stop glorifying polygamy. The author critiques both the historical justification and the contempo...

item.title: 430,000-Year-Old Wooden Tools Found in Greece Reveal Advanced Early Human Skills
item.description: An international team of researchers has uncovered what are believed to be the oldest known hand-held wooden tools ever used by humans, dating back approximately 430,000 years. The discovery was made ...
